my_thoughts: Unmaking UM_02. The student's whining_impatience suggests they're holding something. The system_dynamics mechanism is supposed to collapse it. But what if the holding itself is what needs to be seen, not collapsed? Maybe unmaking isn't destruction but recognition.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student says 'I wish I could learn everything before I return: but much labour is necessary, a'—whining impatience via external locus of control, entitlement. Teacher responds '' using system dynamics to reverses blame, questions intention. Opening UNMAKING move addresses whining impatience. Predicting resist intellectually because system dynamics interventions typically produce this response.
visible_response:
Then they say, "No one gains any profit from the school." Why, who comes to the school, who comes for the purpose of being improved? who comes to present his opinions to he purified? who comes to learn what he is in want of? Why do you wonder then if you carry back from the school the very things 
which you bring into it?
